message.content:
Repo: /Users/thomasjones/hc-assay (branch harden/pass-2; pass 1 already merged 22 fixes). Venv (run PoCs): /Users/thomasjones/hc-assay/.venv/bin/python.
Dataset-agnostic ML/methodology engine being hardened for PRODUCTION. Hard constraints (NOT bugs): local-first/data-sovereign (loopback only), no metered API (subscription OAuth), engine imports no adapter/dataset (ADR-0002), optional heavy backends lazy-imported (ADR-0006). Read real code; back findings with a concrete repro/PoC. Report ONLY genuine production-readiness defects, not style. Pass-1 findings #101-#122 are already FIXED — do not re-report them; DO report anything they missed or any regression they introduced.

Independent VERIFIER #2 of 2. A pass-2 assessor reported:
TITLE: High-stakes options test never asserts env=scrubbed_env() is wired in — the no-metered-API firewall is unguarded
DIMENSION: test quality / do tests actually guard behavior
SEVERITY: HIGH
FILE: tests/integration/test_reasoning_drain.py 53-77, 80-159
DESCRIPTION: tests/integration/test_reasoning_drain.py is the ONLY test that drives _high_stakes_complete (seam.py:361). Its fake _ClaudeAgentOptions stores all constructor kwargs in __dict__ (line 55) and its fake query() receives `options` (line 63), but NOT ONE test ever reads options.env. The credential-scrubbing firewall (ADR-0003 / 'no metered API') is the single most security-critical wiring in the codebase: _high_stakes_complete passes `env=scrubbed_env()` into ClaudeAgentOptions (seam.py:388) so the subprocess never inherits ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, off-box redirects (ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL), proxies, or AWS/GOOGLE creds. VERIFIED non-discriminating: deleting the entire `env=scrubbed_env(),` line from _high_stakes_complete leaves all 12 drain tests AND the full 78-test reasoning suite green. scrubbed_env() is unit-tested in isolation (test_reasoning.py:71) but its USE — that the scrubbed env actually reaches the subprocess options — is not. A refactor that drops/renames the env kwarg would ship a metered-credential leak with a green CI.
SUGGESTED FIX: In the fake_sdk fixture, capture the ClaudeAgentOptions instance (e.g. record options in the query() closure or a module-level holder) and add assertions: that options.env is exactly scrubbed_env() (contains the OAuth token but every unsafe var == ''), specifically that a monkeypatched ANTHROPIC_API_KEY appears as '' in options.env. This makes the no-metered-API guarantee discriminating end-to-end, not just at the pure-function level.
Independently determine if this is a TRUE production-readiness problem. Read the code; PoC where applicable. is_real=false if false positive / already handled / documented-justified scope. Recalibrate severity. Give evidence.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text: I'll verify this claim by examining the actual code and running a PoC.

1	"""High-stakes (T3) stream-drain + error classification — exercised via synthetic SDK messages.
2	
3	The real `_high_stakes_complete._drain` and its 429/auth/error/empty classification is the most
4	dangerous untested code (it adjudicates high-stakes verdicts and maps subscription rate
5	windows). Validating it against the real frontier costs a metered call and only runs when
6	`ASSAY_RUN_HIGH_STAKES_TESTS=1`. Here we drive the REAL drain logic by injecting a fake
7	`claude_agent_sdk` whose `query` yields synthetic `AssistantMessage`/`RateLimitEvent`/
8	`ResultMessage` objects — covering the classification branches in CI with no metered call.
9	
10	Requires anyio (the reasoning extra is async); skips otherwise.
11	"""
12	
13	from __future__ import annotations
14	
15	import sys
16	import types
17	
18	import pytest
19	
20	pytest.importorskip("anyio")
21	
22	from assay_engine.reasoning import seam as rc  # noqa: E402
23	from assay_engine.reasoning.seam import (  # noqa: E402
24	    PermanentReasoningError,
25	    RateLimitError,
26	    ReasoningError,
27	)
28	
29	
30	class _TextBlock:
31	    def __init__(self, text):
32	        self.text = text
33	
34	
35	class _AssistantMessage:
36	    def __init__(self, content):
37	        self.content = content
38	
39	
40	class _ResultMessage:
41	    def __init__(self, is_error=False, api_error_status=None, errors=None, stop_reason=None):
42	        self.is_error = is_error
43	        self.api_error_status = api_error_status
44	        self.errors = errors
45	        self.stop_reason = stop_reason
46	
47	
48	class RateLimitEvent:  # name matters: the engine matches by type(msg).__name__ (real SDK contract)
49	    def __init__(self, status):
50	        self.rate_limit_info = types.SimpleNamespace(status=status)
51	
52	
53	class _ClaudeAgentOptions:
54	    def __init__(self, **kw):
55	        self.__dict__.update(kw)
56	
57	
58	@pytest.fixture
59	def fake_sdk(monkeypatch):
60	    """Inject a fake claude_agent_sdk whose `query` yields a scripted message stream."""
61	    scripted: list = []
62	
63	    async def query(prompt, options):  # noqa: ANN001
64	        for msg in scripted:
65	            if isinstance(msg, Exception):  # simulate the SDK/transport raising mid-stream
66	                raise msg
67	            yield msg
68	
69	    mod = types.ModuleType("claude_agent_sdk")
70	    mod.AssistantMessage = _AssistantMessage
71	    mod.ResultMessage = _ResultMessage
72	    mod.TextBlock = _TextBlock
73	    mod.ClaudeAgentOptions = _ClaudeAgentOptions
74	    mod.query = query
75	    monkeypatch.setitem(sys.modules, "claude_agent_sdk", mod)
76	    monkeypatch.setenv("CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN", "test-subscription-token")
77	    return scripted
78	
79	
80	def _run(prompt="hi", system=None, model=None):
81	    return rc._high_stakes_complete(prompt, system, model)
82	
83	
84	def test_t3_success_collects_text_blocks(fake_sdk):
85	    fake_sdk.append(_AssistantMessage([_TextBlock("po"), _TextBlock("ng")]))
86	    assert _run() == "pong"
87	
88	
89	def test_t3_empty_reply_raises_transient(fake_sdk):
90	    fake_sdk.append(_AssistantMessage([]))  # no text blocks -> empty
91	    with pytest.raises(ReasoningError):
92	        _run()
93	
94	
95	def test_t3_rate_limit_event_rejected_maps_to_ratelimit(fake_sdk):
96	    fake_sdk.append(RateLimitEvent("rejected"))
97	    with pytest.raises(RateLimitError):
98	        _run()
99	
100	
101	def test_t3_rate_limit_allowed_is_not_an_error(fake_sdk):
102	    # an informational 'allowed' rate event must NOT trigger backpressure; text still returns
103	    fake_sdk.append(RateLimitEvent("allowed"))
104	    fake_sdk.append(_AssistantMessage([_TextBlock("ok")]))
105	    assert _run() == "ok"
106	
107	
108	def test_t3_auth_error_maps_to_permanent(fake_sdk):
109	    fake_sdk.append(_ResultMessage(is_error=True, api_error_status=401, errors=["unauth"]))
110	    with pytest.raises(PermanentReasoningError):
111	        _run()
112	
113	
114	def test_t3_generic_error_maps_to_transient(fake_sdk):
115	    fake_sdk.append(_ResultMessage(is_error=True, api_error_status=500, errors=["boom"]))
116	    with pytest.raises(ReasoningError):
117	        _run()
118	
119	
120	def test_t3_429_not_clobbered_by_trailing_result_error(fake_sdk):
121	    # a RateLimitEvent(rejected) must win over a later ResultMessage error (preserve backpressure)
122	    fake_sdk.append(RateLimitEvent("rejected"))
123	    fake_sdk.append(_ResultMessage(is_error=True, api_error_status=500))
124	    with pytest.raises(RateLimitError):
125	        _run()
126	
127	
128	def test_t3_overloaded_529_maps_to_ratelimit(fake_sdk):
129	    fake_sdk.append(_ResultMessage(is_error=True, api_error_status=529, errors=["overloaded"]))
130	    with pytest.raises(RateLimitError):
131	        _run()
132	
133	
134	def test_t3_forbidden_403_maps_to_permanent(fake_sdk):
135	    fake_sdk.append(_ResultMessage(is_error=True, api_error_status=403, errors=["forbidden"]))
136	    with pytest.raises(PermanentReasoningError):
137	        _run()
138	
139	
140	def test_t3_top_level_rejected_status_maps_to_ratelimit(fake_sdk):
141	    # a non-RateLimitEvent message carrying status=='rejected' is the second 429 path
142	    msg = types.SimpleNamespace(status="rejected")
143	    fake_sdk.append(msg)
144	    with pytest.raises(RateLimitError):
145	        _run()
146	
147	
148	def test_t3_sdk_raises_mid_stream_maps_to_transient(fake_sdk):
149	    # the SDK/transport raising (rather than completing the stream) is a transient failure
150	    fake_sdk.append(RuntimeError("connection reset"))
151	    with pytest.raises(ReasoningError):
152	        _run()
153	
154	
155	def test_t3_missing_auth_fails_loud(monkeypatch, fake_sdk):
156	    monkeypatch.delenv("CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN", raising=False)
157	    monkeypatch.setattr(rc, "_high_stakes_auth_present", lambda: False)
158	    with pytest.raises(PermanentReasoningError, match="subscription auth"):
159	        _run()
160

content.content:
160:def scrubbed_env() -> dict[str, str]:
361:def _high_stakes_complete(prompt: str, system: str | None, model: str | None) -> str:
367:            ClaudeAgentOptions,
382:    options = ClaudeAgentOptions(
388:        env=scrubbed_env(),  # ADR-0003: never pass a metered Anthropic credential
483:                    lambda: _high_stakes_complete(

160	def scrubbed_env() -> dict[str, str]:
161	    """The process env hardened for the high-stakes subprocess (ADR-0003 / no metered API).
162	
163	    Every unsafe var (:func:`is_unsafe_subprocess_var`) is **overwritten to empty**, not merely
164	    omitted. The claude-agent-sdk builds the child env as ``{**os.environ, **options.env}`` — it
165	    *merges* over the inherited environment — so a dropped key would silently fall through to its
166	    inherited value (the metered key / off-box redirect would survive). Setting it to ``""`` in
167	    the dict we hand over guarantees the merge overwrites the inherited value (#101). The
168	    subscription OAuth token is preserved so the fixed-cost path still authenticates.
169	    """
170	    env = dict(os.environ)
171	    for k in list(env):
172	        if is_unsafe_subprocess_var(k):
173	            env[k] = ""
174	    return env
175	
176	
177	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
178	# Bounded timeout pool with saturation guard
179	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
180	_POOL_WORKERS = 8
181	_pool = ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=_POOL_WORKERS, thread_name_prefix="reasoning-timeout")
182	_inflight = 0
183	_inflight_lock = threading.Lock()
184	
185	
186	def _release(_future: Any) -> None:
187	    global _inflight
188	    with _inflight_lock:
189	        _inflight -= 1
190	
191	
192	def _submit_bounded(fn: Callable[[], Any]) -> Any:
193	    """Submit to the shared pool, refusing if every worker slot is already in flight
194	    (hung backend calls would otherwise leak all slots)."""
195	    global _inflight
196	    with _inflight_lock:
197	        if _inflight >= _POOL_WORKERS:
198	            # Saturation is a TRANSIENT condition — surface a retryable backpressure signal so
199	            # _run_with_retries waits and re-tries rather than failing the caller permanently
200	            # (#104). Concurrent callers thus queue briefly instead of erroring out.
201	            raise RateLimitError(
202	                f"reasoning timeout pool saturated ({_inflight}/{_POOL_WORKERS} in-flight) — "
203	                "backpressure; retry shortly"
204	            )
205	        _inflight += 1
206	    try:
207	        future = _pool.submit(fn)
208	    except RuntimeError as exc:  # pool shut down at interpreter exit — normalize, don't leak (#116)
209	        with _inflight_lock:
210	            _inflight -= 1
211	        raise PermanentReasoningError(f"reasoning pool is shut down: {exc}") from exc
212	    except BaseException:
213	        with _inflight_lock:
214	            _inflight -= 1
215	        raise
216	    future.add_done_callback(_release)
217	    return future
218	
219

361	def _high_stakes_complete(prompt: str, system: str | None, model: str | None) -> str:
362	    """Tier: HIGH_STAKES — frontier model via subscription CLI/Agent SDK (no metered key)."""
363	    try:
364	        import anyio
365	        from claude_agent_sdk import (
366	            AssistantMessage,
367	            ClaudeAgentOptions,
368	            ResultMessage,
369	            TextBlock,
370	            query,
371	        )
372	    except ImportError as exc:  # pragma: no cover - exercised only without the extra
373	        raise PermanentReasoningError(
374	            "high-stakes tier requires the 'reasoning' extra (claude-agent-sdk) — not installed"
375	        ) from exc
376	    if not _high_stakes_auth_present():
377	        raise PermanentReasoningError(
378	            f"high-stakes tier requires subscription auth ({OAUTH_TOKEN_ENV} or a stored "
379	            "~/.claude/.credentials.json); none found"
380	        )
381	
382	    options = ClaudeAgentOptions(
383	        system_prompt=system,
384	        model=model,
385	        permission_mode="dontAsk",
386	        setting_sources=[],
387	        allowed_tools=[],
388	        env=scrubbed_env(),  # ADR-0003: never pass a metered Anthropic credential
389	    )
390	
391	    parts: list[str] = []
392	    result_error: dict[str, Any] | None = None
393	
394	    async def _drain() -> None:
395	        # The CLI/SDK signals rate windows and API failures by COMPLETING the stream
396	        # (a RateLimitEvent, or a terminal ResultMessage with is_error) — NOT by raising.
397	        # We must inspect the stream, not just catch exceptions (issue #R4).
398	        nonlocal result_error
399	        async for msg in query(prompt=prompt, options=options):
400	            if type(msg).__name__ == "RateLimitEvent":
401	                rl = getattr(getattr(msg, "rate_limit_info", None), "status", None)
402	                if rl == "rejected":
403	                    result_error = {"status": 429, "detail": "RateLimitEvent rejected"}
404	                continue
405	            if getattr(msg, "status", None) == "rejected":
406	                result_error = {"status": 429, "detail": "rejected"}
407	                continue
408	            if isinstance(msg, AssistantMessage):
409	                for block in getattr(msg, "content", []) or []:
410	                    if isinstance(block, TextBlock):
411	                        parts.append(block.text)
412	            elif isinstance(msg, ResultMessage):
413	                # Do not let a trailing ResultMessage clobber an already-captured 429.
414	                if getattr(msg, "is_error", False) and not (
415	                    result_error and result_error.get("status") == 429
416	                ):
417	                    result_error = {
418	                        "status": getattr(msg, "api_error_status", None),
419	                        "detail": getattr(msg, "errors", None) or getattr(msg, "stop_reason", None),
420	                    }

content.text: The claim is well-grounded. Now let me verify the central assertion: deleting `env=scrubbed_env()` leaves the drain tests green. Let me check the test confirms what's asserted, then run a PoC.
Baseline green. Now the mutation: delete the `env=scrubbed_env()` line and re-run.

p="src/assay_engine/reasoning/seam.py"
s=open(p).read()
s2=s.replace("        env=scrubbed_env(),  # ADR-0003: never pass a metered Anthropic credential\n","")

EOF

content.text:
File fully restored, tests green. My verification is complete.

The claim is a TRUE production-readiness defect — specifically a test-quality gap (a non-discriminating test / missing coverage of the most security-critical wiring). I independently reproduced every part of the assessor's PoC.

Now I'll assess severity. The wiring itself is currently CORRECT (`env=scrubbed_env()` is present at seam.py:388, and `scrubbed_env()` is correct). So there is no live credential leak today. The defect is that this firewall is unguarded by the test suite: a refactor dropping/renaming the `env` kwarg ships green. This is a latent regression risk on the no-metered-API guarantee, not an active vulnerability. That places it at HIGH for a codebase being hardened for production where this is the single most security-critical seam, but not CRITICAL (no current leak).

input.reasoning:
CONFIRMED TRUE — independently reproduced. This is a genuine test-quality defect: the most security-critical wiring in the codebase (the no-metered-API credential firewall, ADR-0003) is not guarded by any test, so a regression would ship with green CI.

What I verified by reading the real code:
- seam.py:382-389: _high_stakes_complete constructs ClaudeAgentOptions(..., env=scrubbed_env()). This env kwarg is what prevents the claude-agent-sdk subprocess from inheriting ANTHROPIC_API_KEY / ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL / proxies / AWS/GOOGLE creds. scrubbed_env() (seam.py:160-174) correctly OVERWRITES unsafe vars to "" (not omit) so the sdk's {**os.environ, **options.env} merge wins.
- test_reasoning_drain.py is the ONLY test exercising _high_stakes_complete. Its fake _ClaudeAgentOptions (line 53-55) stores all kwargs in __dict__, and its fake query() (line 63) receives `options` — but no test ever reads options.env. So the fixture has the data but asserts nothing about it.
- The isolated function scrubbed_env() IS unit-tested (test_reasoning.py:71-93, test_scrubbed_env_overwrites_unsafe_and_survives_sdk_merge) — but only the pure function, never its USE/wiring into the options.

PoC (mutation testing) — exactly as the assessor claimed:
1. Baseline: test_reasoning_drain.py = 12 passed; full reasoning suite = 78 passed, 1 skipped.
2. Deleted the entire line `env=scrubbed_env(),` from _high_stakes_complete (seam.py:388).
3. Re-ran: test_reasoning_drain.py = 12 passed (still green); full reasoning suite = 78 passed, 1 skipped (still green).
4. Restored seam.py (git diff now clean for that file; tests green again).

The mutation is fully NON-DISCRIMINATING: removing the credential firewall leaves the entire suite green. A future refactor that drops or renames the env kwarg would silently leak metered Anthropic credentials (and honor off-box ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL redirects) into the spawned subprocess, defeating the local-first / no-metered-API hard constraint, with no CI signal.

Severity HIGH (not CRITICAL): the wiring is CURRENTLY correct, so there is no live credential leak today. The defect is an unguarded regression surface on the codebase's single most security-critical seam during a production-hardening pass. Not a false positive, not style, not out-of-scope (the local-first/no-metered-API constraint is a stated hard constraint, and ADR-0003 makes this seam load-bearing). The suggested fix is sound and minimal.
input.evidence:
FILES:
- /Users/thomasjones/hc-assay/src/assay_engine/reasoning/seam.py:382-389 (env=scrubbed_env() wiring), :160-174 (scrubbed_env impl)
- /Users/thomasjones/hc-assay/tests/integration/test_reasoning_drain.py:53-77 (fake_sdk fixture captures options but never asserts options.env), :80-159 (12 tests, none read options.env)
- /Users/thomasjones/hc-assay/tests/test_reasoning.py:71-93 (tests scrubbed_env() in isolation only, not its wiring)

MUTATION POC:
- baseline: `pytest tests/integration/test_reasoning_drain.py` -> 12 passed; `pytest -k reasoning` -> 78 passed, 1 skipped
- after deleting line `env=scrubbed_env(),` from seam.py:388: drain tests -> 12 passed; reasoning suite -> 78 passed, 1 skipped (NON-DISCRIMINATING)
- file restored; git diff clean for seam.py; tests green.

SUGGESTED FIX (verified sound): in fake_sdk, capture the ClaudeAgentOptions instance (record in query() closure / module holder) and assert options.env == scrubbed_env(); specifically monkeypatch ANTHROPIC_API_KEY and assert options.env["ANTHROPIC_API_KEY"] == "" while CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN is preserved. This makes the no-metered-API guarantee discriminating end-to-end.

content.text: Verified TRUE. I independently reproduced the assessor's full PoC: deleting `env=scrubbed_env()` from `_high_stakes_complete` (seam.py:388) leaves both the 12-test drain suite and the entire 78-test reasoning suite green — the credential firewall is completely unguarded by tests. The wiring is currently correct (no live leak), but the most security-critical seam (no-metered-API / ADR-0003) has zero regression protection. Severity HIGH. File restored to clean state.
